title: Pass newly-added --log-level flag to Conmon
body: This may require a new Conmon commit in the tests - will know once they run

comments.body:
Can we add the version requirement on conmon to the release notes? Currently, the openSUSE packages go the lazy path just require CRI-O to be installed. I was hoping for a dedicated conmon project to ease packaging, but I think it's time to have a dedicated podman-conmon package in openSUSE.

**EDIT** Or we can do something similar to Docker and print the expected and received version of depending tools (e.g., runc).

@vrothberg We added a version flag to `conmon`, but we've never actually bothered bumping it since then when we made breaking changes... I'd love to be able to use that to say that our conmon is too old, though.
@mheon CI has a similar problem: `option parsing failed: Unknown option --log-level`
@mheon What's your take on breaking conmon out into a dedicated project? I volunteer as a lobbyist if needed ;-)

> [...] I'd love to be able to use that to say that our conmon is too old, though.

@mheon I'd be a bit more radical and specify exactly which version (or commit) libpod expects from conmon. This would increase work for maintainers here but it would also avoid regressions for users.
The CI failures are a bad `conmon` commit - need to update the one we're using.

@vrothberg I think breaking `conmon` out is good decision - it doesn't really make much sense to keep in CRI-O anymore.

I actually didn't notice this conmon change until it landed, so when we bumped the package in Fedora with the new Podman release, we weren't passing the flag, which caused segfaults in conmon. We really need Podman tests on new Conmon commits.
@mrunalp WDYT about breaking conmon out of CRI-O into its own package, Say github.com/containers/conmon

Alright, conmon commit seems to have fixed things.

I agree with @vrothberg that we should advise on the current required conmon commit somewhere, but I'm not sure where is best. We could use the README, but the instructions for building Conmon are in the tutorial.

:pushpin: Commit 68f21cc has been approved by `rhatdan`
